mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it easier to run machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing resources.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay repository.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay --create-namespace --version 1.5.1

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay operator is running correctly by listing the pods in the KubeRay namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> You should see the KubeRay operator pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Ray clusters and jobs managed by KubeRay, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade KubeRay to the latest version while ensuring the operator is not actively managing any Ray clusters.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -r kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay --version 1.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Drains all nodes running Ray clusters.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -r kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD installation may fail due to network issues or incorrect URLs.

**Fix:**
Check your network connection and verify the CRD URLs are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeRay operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayCluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayCluster resource was not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Ensure the RayCluster YAML is correctly defined and apply it again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the RBAC settings and ensure the service account has the correct roles.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new RayJob features, improved CRD validation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when you execute 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
